what: M=64 two-phase: quant + _gemm_afp4wfp4_preshuffle_kernel (A4W4 preshuffle with XCD remapping) instead of fused _gemm_a16wfp4_preshuffle_kernel. A4W4 preshuffle kernel has remap_xcd for 8 XCDs, .wt output stores, no in-loop quant overhead. For 64x7168x2048: quant is fast (64 rows), XCD remap distributes 224 blocks across 8 XCDs for better L2 locality. v237 tried two-phase with ASM GEMM (+0.7%), v135 tried _preshuffle_scales (different kernel, non-preshuffle B, M=256). _gemm_afp4wfp4_preshuffle_kernel with preshuffle B never tried for M=64.
why: The _gemm_a16wfp4_preshuffle_kernel used for fused path lacks XCD remapping, while _gemm_afp4wfp4_preshuffle_kernel has it. MI355X has 8 XCDs and the XCD remap distributes work better across them. M=64 at 13.3us is the biggest contributor to geomean.
